channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Newcastle confident ahead of Champions League last-16 draw after strong European run
item.description: Newcastle United have sent a clear message of confidence to potential Champions League opponents after securing their place in the last 16 with a 3-2 win over Qarabag, completing a dominant 9-3 aggreg...

item.title: UK Government Reopens Revised Sustainable Farming Incentive with Funding Caps and Reforms
item.description: The UK government has announced the relaunch of its flagship environmental payment programme for farmers, the Sustainable Farming Incentive (SFI), after its abrupt closure in March 2025 caused widespr...

item.title: Upcoming Webinar Critiques Canada's Small Modular Reactor Initiatives and Spending
item.description: A free webinar titled 'An Assessment of SMR Projects: The Case of Canada' is scheduled for Wednesday, March 18, 2026, at 1:00 PM EST (2:00 PM Atlantic Time). Hosted by Nuclear Transparency Watch based...

item.title: Polanski and Farage: Surprising Parallels Despite Political Differences
item.description: Zack Polanski of the Green Party and Nigel Farage of Reform UK may seem like polar opposites in terms of politics, values, and lifestyles, yet they share some striking similarities. Both have been dis...

item.title: Trump Faces Limited Options as Pressure on Iran Fails
item.description: The United States, under President Donald Trump, has amassed significant military forces near Iran in an effort to compel the Iranian government to negotiate on terms favorable to Washington. Despite ...

item.title: Trump's Major Military Action in Iran: Risks and Uncertain Outcome
item.description: President Donald Trump's decision to launch 'Operation Epic Fury' against Iran represents one of the most significant gambles made by an American president since the Iraq War. Initially elected on a p...

item.title: Man pleads guilty to theft of £2m Fabergé egg, watch and fraud charges in London
item.description: A 29-year-old man has admitted in court to stealing a handbag containing a Fabergé egg and a watch believed to be worth more than £2 million. Enzo Conticello, who is also known as Hakin Boudjenoune an...

item.title: Noah B. Taylor Exits ‘Wednesday’ as Joanna Lumley Becomes Series Regular for Season 3
item.description: Netflix’s “Wednesday” is undergoing notable cast changes ahead of its third season. Noah B. Taylor, who joined the series in Season 2 as Bruno Yuson, will not return as a series regular. His character...

item.title: Best Fashion Looks from the 2026 Brit Awards Red Carpet
item.description: The Brit Awards 2026, held at the Co-op Live arena in Manchester, celebrated the finest in British music, but the red carpet was the real showstopper. Celebrities like Harry Styles, Olivia Dean, Sam F...

item.title: Nigeria rejects claims of ransom payment in St Mary's school abduction
item.description: The Nigerian government has strongly denied claims by AFP that it paid a large ransom to Boko Haram for the release of over 200 pupils and staff abducted from St Mary's School in Papiri, Niger State, ...

item.title: Nearly a Quarter of English Councils Unable to Meet Weekly Food Waste Collection Deadline
item.description: A BBC investigation has found that almost a quarter of councils in England will not meet the upcoming government deadline to provide weekly food waste collections to all households. The Department for...

item.title: Xiaomi unveils new 17 Ultra smartphone, AirTag clone, and slim power bank at MWC 2026
item.description: Xiaomi has launched several new devices at the 2026 Mobile World Congress in Barcelona, including its flagship 17 Ultra smartphone, a new AirTag-like tracker, an ultra-slim power bank, and the Xiaomi ...

item.title: Supreme Court Case Challenges Gun Ownership for Marijuana Users Amid Unlikely Alliances
item.description: A case before the US Supreme Court is raising the question of whether marijuana users should be allowed to legally own firearms. The case involves a federal law that bars habitual marijuana users from...

item.title: Fears of Renewed Violence in Guadalajara After CJNG Rampage and Killing of El Mencho
item.description: Guadalajara, in Mexico’s western state of Jalisco, is grappling with the aftermath of a violent rampage carried out by the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG) following the reported killing of its le...

item.title: How Renewable Energy Is Shaping the Future of Power Generation
item.description: The article discusses how the growing integration of renewable energy is reshaping traditional energy systems. In particular, it highlights efforts by China and France to retrofit coal and nuclear pla...

item.title: Inquiry reveals police failures in arresting Nottingham triple killer before attacks
item.description: An inquiry has revealed serious lapses by Nottinghamshire and Leicestershire police in handling Valdo Calocane, who killed three people in Nottingham in June 2023 and injured three others. An arrest w...

item.title: Flights from the UK to the Middle East disrupted as Dubai and Doha airports halt services amid growing tensions
item.description: Flights from the UK to destinations across the Middle East have been severely disrupted as airports in Dubai and Doha suspend all services. The suspension follows escalating tensions in the region aft...

item.title: NHS obtains alternative bone cement to continue joint surgeries
item.description: The NHS has successfully secured a supply of alternative bone cement, allowing postponed joint surgeries, including hip and knee replacements, to resume. Last week, hospitals were instructed to delay ...

item.title: Powerful Nor’easter Leaves Two Dead and Causes Major Disruptions Across US Northeast
item.description: A powerful winter storm swept across the northeastern United States, leaving at least two people dead and causing widespread disruption across several states. In Calvert County, Maryland, a tragic acc...

item.title: UK Introduces Mandatory Electronic Travel Authorisation System, Creating Challenges for Dual Nationals
item.description: The UK has introduced a new Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) system that will become mandatory from 25 February for visitors from 85 countries who previously did not require a visa for short stay...
